input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## Step 1 — Install the .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# Install MAUI (pulls in Android SDK, build tools, etc.)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows target requires the Windows App SDK and WinUI tooling. The simplest path:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or, for build-tools only (CI/headless):
# winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required VS workload:

```powershell
# Run from an elevated prompt
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installershell.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --quiet --norestart
```

## Step 4 — Android SDK Setup

The MAUI workload auto-installs an Android SDK, but you may need to accept licenses and discover what was installed:

```powershell
# Find the auto-provisioned Android SDK location
$sdkRoot = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
# Fallback: check the dotnet android manifest
dotnet android sdk info 2>$null

# List installed SDK platforms & build-tools dynamically
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Accept all licenses
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# Install a specific API level if needed (discover latest first)
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0"
```

Set the environment variable so tools can find it:

```powershell
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $sdkRoot, "User")
```

## Step 5 — Java (OpenJDK)

MAUI bundles Microsoft OpenJDK. Verify it's present:

```powershell
# The workload usually installs it here:
$jdkPath = Get-ChildItem "$env:ProgramFiles\Microsoft\jdk-*" -Directory |
  Sort-Object Name -Descending | Select-Object -First 1

# Verify
& "$($jdkPath.FullName)\bin\java.exe" -version

# Set JAVA_HOME if not already set
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME", $jdkPath.FullName, "User")
```

## Step 6 — Android Emulator (optional)

```powershell
# Ensure Hyper-V / WHPX is enabled (elevated prompt)
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All -NoRestart

# Create an AVD dynamically using the latest system image
$sysImg = & "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>&1 |
  Select-String "system-images;android-\d+;google_apis;x86_64" |
  ForEach-Object { ($_ -split '\s+')[0] } |
  Sort-Object -Descending | Select-Object -First 1

& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "$sysImg"

& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "maui_emulator" -k "$sysImg" --device "pixel_5" --force
```

## Step 7 — Enable Developer Mode (Windows target)

```powershell
# Elevated prompt
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1
```

## Step 8 — Create and Build a Test Project

```powershell
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest
cd MauiTest

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
& "$sdkRoot\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd maui_emulator &
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## Step 9 — Discover TFMs Dynamically

If you're unsure which target framework monikers are available:

```powershell
# List available MAUI templates and their TFMs
dotnet new maui --dry-run -n Dummy 2>$null
# Or inspect an existing project:
Select-String -Path *.csproj -Pattern "TargetFrameworks" |
  ForEach-Object { $_.Line.Trim() }
```

---

**Checklist summary:** `winget` → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → VS workload for WinUI → accept Android licenses → set `ANDROID_HOME` / `JAVA_HOME` → enable Developer Mode → build & run. All version discovery is dynamic via `sdkmanager --list`, `Get-ChildItem`, and `dotnet workload list`.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands now and want all the PowerShell commands ready to go. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
